cards.front: A 4-bit full adder consists of 4 1-bit adders, one for each bit position.
cards.back: A 4-bit full adder is a digital circuit that adds four binary digits (bits) and produces a sum and carry-out value.

cards.front: A 4-bit full subtractor consists of 4 1-bit subtractors, one for each bit position.
cards.back: A 4-bit full subtractor is a digital circuit that subtracts four binary digits (bits) and produces a difference and borrow-out value.

cards.front: To design a 4-bit full adder, we need 2-bit full adders for each bit position.
cards.back: A 2-bit full adder is a digital circuit that adds two binary digits (bits) and produces a sum and carry-out value. We use these to design a 4-bit full adder.

cards.front: To design a 4-bit full subtractor, we need 2-bit full subtractors for each bit position.
cards.back: A 2-bit full subtractor is a digital circuit that subtracts two binary digits (bits) and produces a difference and borrow-out value. We use these to design a 4-bit full subtractor.

cards.front: A 4-bit full adder and subtractor can be simulated using basic gates (AND, OR, NOT, and XOR) using Karnaugh maps.
cards.back: Karnaugh maps are a graphical method for simplifying digital logic expressions. They can be used to design and simulate digital circuits using basic gates.
